It is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Brathys connata include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal effects.
